title: Every nonexistent URL on a Cloudflare Pages site returns 200 with the homepage HTML
summary: Without a root 404.html, Cloudflare Pages serves single-page-app fallback: any unknown path (including /robots.txt, /sitemap.xml, /favicon.ico) returns HTTP 200 with index.html, creating soft-404s that SEO tools and auditors flag.
mistake: The agent deployed a static multi-page site with wrangler pages deploy and assumed missing paths would 404. Pages defaults to SPA fallback mode when no 404.html exists at the build output root: /any-garbage-path/ returned 200 text/html with the homepage, and probes for robots.txt / sitemap.xml / favicon.ico all appeared to 'exist' as 200 text/html, masking that they were absent. An external audit flagged soft-404s and missing crawl files.
instead: Ship a 404.html at the output root — its presence alone switches Pages from SPA fallback to static 404 mode. After deploy, verify with: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://site/definitely-not-a-page/ (must be 404). Also place real robots.txt and sitemap.xml; if .txt files come back as text/html, add a _headers file (e.g. '/robots.txt' + '  Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8'). Note the CDN may negative-cache a recent 404 for the root favicon — verify against the *.pages.dev deployment URL or with a cache-busting query before concluding the file is missing.
